title: Roghni Naan

seo.title: Roghni Naan Recipe: Authentic Pakistani Flatbread
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Roghni Naan, the delicious Pakistani flatbread. This easy-to-follow recipe brings the taste of Pakistan to your home. Perfect for any meal!
seo.imageAlt: Freshly baked authentic Roghni Naan, a traditional Pakistani flatbread, served warm and garnished.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy the soft, chewy texture and rich flavor of homemade Roghni Naan, a staple in Pakistani cuisine.

description: Roghni Naan is a rich, flavorful Pakistani flatbread, traditionally baked in a tandoor. Its soft, chewy texture, often brushed with butter or ghee, makes it a perfect accompaniment to any curry or stew. Adorned with sesame seeds and sometimes nigella seeds, it offers a delightful aromatic experience with every bite.

instructions.title: Activate Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine warm milk, sugar, and active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until frothy. This indicates the yeast is active and ready.

instructions.title: Combine Dry Ingredients
instructions.text: In a separate large bowl, whisk together the sifted all-purpose flour and salt. Create a well in the center of the flour mixture.

instructions.title: Prepare Dough
instructions.text: Pour the activated yeast mixture, plain yogurt, beaten egg, and 2 tablespoons of melted ghee or butter into the well of the dry ingredients. Mix with a spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms.

instructions.title: Knead the Dough
instructions.text: Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until it becomes smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky. If the dough is too dry, add a tiny bit of warm water; if too sticky, add a sprinkle of flour.

instructions.title: First Rise
instructions.text: Lightly grease a clean bowl with oil. Place the kneaded dough in the bowl, turning it once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Shape and Top Naans
instructions.text: Once risen, gently punch down the dough and divide it into 4-6 equal portions. Roll each portion into an oval or round shape, about 1/4 inch thick. Press your fingertips gently to create indentations on the surface. Sprinkle generously with sesame seeds and optional nigella seeds.

instructions.title: Bake Naans
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to its highest setting (around 450-500°F or 230-260°C) with a baking stone or inverted baking sheet inside for at least 20 minutes. Carefully place a naan onto the hot surface. Bake for 3-5 minutes, or until puffed up and lightly golden brown with some charred spots.

instructions.title: Serve Warm
instructions.text: Remove the hot naan from the oven and immediately brush with extra melted ghee or butter. Serve hot with your favorite Pakistani curries, kebabs, or raita.

tips: For a truly authentic flavor, try baking the naan on a preheated pizza stone or inverted baking sheet in a very hot oven to mimic a tandoor.
Ensure your warm milk is not too hot, as it can kill the yeast, or too cold, which will not activate it properly. Aim for lukewarm.
Roghni Naan is best enjoyed fresh and warm. If you have leftovers, lightly sprinkle with water, wrap in foil, and reheat in a warm oven.
article: Roghni Naan is a beloved Pakistani Baking dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
